texts.full_text: emical incidents. The LEPC is composed of representatives from local government, emergency services, industry, and community groups. Their responsibilities include: 1. Gathering and disseminating information about hazardous chemicals and potential hazards within the community. 2. Developing and maintaining an up-to-date Community Emergency Response Plan (CERP) that outlines procedures for responding to chemical emergencies. 3. Conducting public education and outreach to inform residents about the presence of hazardous materials in their area and how to respond in case of an emergency. 4. Participating in the Emergency planning and Community
